title: Gas report - Week 8
summary:
LNG
The LNG spot market saw a strong week, with the Atlantic firming and the Pacific remaining flat. With a few uncovered cargoes in the West and a tightening tonnage list at the front end of the fixing window, it led to an upward correction in rates. On the BLNG1 Australia-Japan route, 174k cbm vessels slipped $400 to $27,400/day.

children.title: LNG
children.summary: The LNG spot market saw a strong week, with the Atlantic firming and the Pacific remaining flat. With a few uncovered cargoes in the West and a tightening tonnage list at the front end of the fixing window, it led to an upward correction in rates.

children.title: LPG
children.summary: The LPG market regained momentum this week as participants gradually returned following the Lunar New Year holidays in the East. Improved enquiry and a pickup in fixing activity lent firmer undertones across all major routes, particularly toward the end of the week.
